statement.markdown: Given a doubly linked list and a new node with a given value, insert the new node before the head of the list. The list is represented as a sequence of node values. The new node value is also given. The task is to output the modified list after the insertion. The head of the list is the first node in the sequence. The output should be the sequence of node values in the modified list.
statement.inputFormat: The first line contains the number of nodes in the list. The second line contains the values of the nodes in the list. The third line contains the value of the new node to be inserted.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the sequence of node values in the modified list, one value per line, with the count of nodes on the line immediately before the sequence.
statement.notes: The input list can be empty. The new node should be inserted before the head of the list, if the list is not empty. If the list is empty, the new node becomes the head of the list.

constraints.description: number of nodes in the list
values of the nodes in the list
value of the new node to be inserted

examples.explanation: Insert the new node with value 4 before the head of the list [1, 2, 3]. The modified list is [4, 1, 2, 3].
Insert the new node with value 5 into an empty list. The modified list is [5].

hints.text: Consider the structure of a doubly linked list and how nodes are connected. The head node is the first node in the list.
Think about how to insert a new node before the head node. You will need to update the next pointer of the head node to point to the new node.

To insert the new node before the head node, you will also need to update the prev pointer of the new node to point to a dummy node (a node that is not part of the original list). This dummy node will help you to maintain the correct order of the nodes in the list.
The dummy node will be the new head of the list after the insertion. You can use the dummy node to simplify the process of updating the next and prev pointers of the nodes in the list.
Use the dummy node to create a new linked list with the new node inserted before the head node. Then, return the values of the nodes in the new linked list.

editorial.title: Insert Before Head in DLL
editorial.intuition: The problem requires inserting a new node before the head of a doubly linked list. The brute force approach involves traversing the list to find the head, then updating the head and the new node's pointers. However, this can be optimized by simply adding the new node at the beginning of the list and updating the head.
editorial.bruteForce: The brute force solution is correct but slow because it involves unnecessary traversals of the list to find the head. This approach has a time complexity of O(n) due to the traversal, but it is not efficient for large lists.
editorial.betterSolution: A better approach would be to directly insert the new node at the beginning of the list without traversing it.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves directly inserting the new node at the beginning of the list and updating the head. This approach has a time complexity of O(n) because we still need to print the updated list, but it is more efficient than the brute force approach because it avoids unnecessary traversals.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the optimal solution can be proven by showing that the new node is correctly inserted at the beginning of the list and the head is updated. This can be verified by checking the output of the algorithm, which should be the updated list with the new node at the beginning.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run of the algorithm can be performed by manually inserting a new node at the beginning of a sample list and verifying that the output is correct.
commonMistakes: Forgetting to update the head of the list after inserting the new node
Incorrectly updating the pointers of the new node
Failing to handle the case where the list is empty
Not printing the updated list correctly

functionSignature.java: public List<Integer> insertBeforeHead(List<Integer> nums, int newVal);
functionSignature.python: def insertBeforeHead(self, nums: list[int], newVal: int) -> list[int]:
